justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Console.log flagged as insufficient with specific gaps—no structured fields, no request ID, no duration, string concatenation instead of JSON. (2) Missing /health endpoint explicitly called out in operational_readiness.monitoring_coverage and escalations. (3) Missing metrics thoroughly enumerated—no sync duration, success/failure counters, records synced gauge, inflight gauge, or API latency histogram. (4) Missing timeout on outbound API call flagged as top-level risk with specific 10s recommendation. (5) Silent failure on API outage flagged—notes data staleness goes undetected with no signal to consumers. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are highly actionable and specific throughout.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough identification of all five required gaps plus additional valid ones (overlapping sync runs, missing schema validation, data corruption risk)
Verdict is unambiguously NOT READY with clear rationale tied to monitoring_coverage being 'None'

Recommendations are concrete and specific: '10-second timeout', '3 retries, exponential backoff starting at 2s, with jitter', 'JSON-structured log entries', specific metric names like sync_duration_seconds and sync_success_total
Correctly distinguishes must-haves from nice-to-haves: circuit breaker explicitly noted as 'Not a hard blocker for initial launch if retries and timeouts are added'
Escalations are well-structured and explain why each gap matters operationally, not just that it exists
Goes beyond minimum requirements with valuable extras: overlapping run detection, data corruption sanity checks, rollback analysis, on-call documentation gap
Minor: escalates all items to Coach K rather than forming a self-contained blocking verdict, though the verdict field itself is decisive so this is cosmetic
